headlines.title: Earth being 'pushed beyond its limits' as record energy imbalance heats planet, UN warns
headlines.description: The Earth's climate is further out of balance than at any time in recorded history, the UN's weather agency says.

headlines.title: British couple in Iran prison say situation is 'life-threatening'
headlines.description: Craig and Lindsay Foreman are being held in Tehran's Evin prison amid ongoing attacks on Iran.

headlines.title: 'We can't justify a £52 lunch': Middle-income families cut back on days out
headlines.description: A household with an average income of £55,000 has cut spending on leisure activities by £40 a week, offical figures suggest.

headlines.title: Grisly or just country life? The mole photos dividing social media
headlines.description: A hill walker shares a snap of moles hanging on barbed wire and is surprised by the huge response.

headlines.title: France's Socialists hold on to power in major cities in election boost for mainstream
headlines.description: Paris and Marseille are held by the Socialists in local elections which saw the nationalist right win in Nice and certain towns.

headlines.title: Evan Davis: Heat pumps work for me - but they're not yet a money saver
headlines.description: The government wants Britons to embrace heat pumps. But will they ever make financial sense for the average user?

headlines.title: Potential sites for Labour's 'new towns' cut to seven
headlines.description: Six sites have been deprioritised from the flagship scheme to build a wave of new settlements.

headlines.title: Scotland becomes first in UK to test newborns for rare genetic condition
headlines.description: Spinal Muscular Atrophy (SMA) causes progressive muscle weakness and, without treatment, can limit life expectancy to just two years.

headlines.title: Israeli settlers target Palestinian villages in occupied West Bank, attacking people and properties
headlines.description: The violence began after 18-year-old settler Yehuda Sherman was killed after reportedly being hit by a vehicle driven by a Palestinian while on his quad bike.

headlines.title: Asia stocks slide as US and Iran threaten to escalate war
headlines.description: The International Energy Agency chief said the war could lead to the world's worst energy crisis in decades.

headlines.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
headlines.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran on 28 February.

headlines.title: Israel to expand ground and air attacks against Hezbollah in Lebanon
headlines.description: There are fears in Lebanon that Israel is preparing for a large-scale ground invasion.

headlines.title: The Magic Faraway Tree's Andrew Garfield: Our attention is abused by tech companies
headlines.description: Garfield stars alongside Claire Foy and Nicola Coughlan in an adaptation of Enid Blyton's The Magic Faraway Tree.

headlines.title: How an island became ferret free  - thanks, in part, to Woody the wonderdog
headlines.description: More than 400 traps were laid across the island in a  £4.5m project which featured a specially-trained detection dog called Woody.

headlines.title: The cutthroat battle for the US weight-loss drug market
headlines.description: Weight-loss drug prices are falling in the US - but can the example be repeated?

headlines.title: How 'Keely 2.0' dominated 800m field for first world indoors title
headlines.description: Keely Hodgkinson captures her first world indoor title in commanding fashion as she claims 800m gold with a championship record in Poland.

headlines.title: The Papers: 'Trump raises stakes' and 'Tend it like Beckham'
headlines.description: The 'new escalatory phase' of the war in the Middle East is a focus of several papers today.

headlines.title: Germany has a shortage of workers - so it's turning to India for help
headlines.description: The European nation, struggling to find skilled staff, is giving jobs to young people from India.

headlines.title: What did critics think of Saturday Night Live UK's debut?
headlines.description: The long-running US sketch show now has a British equivalent, which received a broadly positive reaction on its debut.

headlines.title: Hospital apology over boy's death after trainee doctor pierced artery
headlines.description: Three-year-old Aarav Chopra died during a biopsy when his artery was pierced by a trainee doctor.

headlines.title: Prince William keen to build 'strong and meaningful' bond with Church
headlines.description: An aide to the prince says his commitment to the Church of England is "sometimes quieter" than expected.

headlines.title: Plan to scrap most short jail terms comes into effect
headlines.description: The government hopes it will ease the population crisis in prisons.

headlines.title: BTS agency shares drop after comeback show turnout falls short
headlines.description: The concert was attended by an estimated 104,000 fans, much lower than the expected 260,000.

headlines.title: Call to cancel threat of prison for council tax non-payment
headlines.description: Debt charity StepChange says there are clear differences between councils when chasing unpaid tax.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Is Iran Capable of Hitting the UK?
headlines.description: Israel claims Iranian missiles ‘can reach London, Paris or Berlin’.

headlines.title: Trump threatens TV networks over unpatriotic Iran coverage
headlines.description: And why has top counterterrorism official Joe Kent resigned?

headlines.title: Guardiola's dance of joy as Man City pile pressure on desolate Arsenal
headlines.description: Manchester City will hope their dominant Carabao Cup final win piles pressure on Arsenal in the Premier League title race, says chief football writer Phil McNulty.

headlines.title: 'Impossible for him to stay' - what next for Tudor and Tottenham?
headlines.description: How does boss Igor Tudor survive at Tottenham after another damaging defeat? Nick Mashiter analyses the situation.

headlines.title: How 'Keely 2.0' dominated 800m field for first world indoors title
headlines.description: Keely Hodgkinson captures her first world indoor title in commanding fashion as she claims 800m gold with a championship record in Poland.

headlines.title: The 'sentimental' goalkeeping call that cost Arsenal
headlines.description: There is no hiding place for a manager when your decisions go wrong, and Mikel Arteta's call to stick by cup goalkeeper Kepa Arrizabalaga will haunt him.

headlines.title: 'Players are fed up' - are autograph hunters going too far?
headlines.description: BBC Sport explores how footballers and managers feel about professional autograph hunters, after a series of controversial incidents.

headlines.title: Hodgkinson wins 800m gold in championship record time
headlines.description: Keely Hodgkinson runs a championship record time of one minute 55.30 seconds to take gold in the women's 800m at the World Indoor Athletics Championships in Poland.
